title: ka ,いｋ活用できるか？とkにCursorversa
summary_text: Kernel orchestration is active for this session. Bootstrap target: 6+ lanes (minimum 6). Active models: Codex, GLM, Gemini. Lane manifest: - `session:87322`: GLM review lane - availability probe succeeded for Cursorvers backend/orchestration evaluation - `session:75665`: GLM architecture lane - concluded Google AI agent tooling should be an orchestration layer above the existing backend - `session:2568`: GLM integration lane - identified read-only/context layers as safe first integration points and mutation paths as deterministic boundaries - `session:7576`: Gemini fallback lane - availability probe succeeded as a live non-Codex reviewer lane - `session:19021`: Gemini architecture lane - confirmed orchestration-layer placement over backend-of-record - `session:57713`: Gemini integration lane - identified safe assistive entry points and explicit human-gated deterministic boundaries 評価は変わりません。`Cursorvers.Inc` で Google の AI agent tooling を使うなら、`backend-of-record` ではなく `orchestration / reasoning layer` として使うべきです。既存の deterministic backend を正本に残し、agent は判断、既存 API/Function は実行、という分離が最も安全です。 実装方針はシンプルです。 - 先に AI を入れる場所: 読み取り専用の文脈理解、意図解釈、要約、生成 - 後回しにする場所: managed memory/session や本番 agent runtime の深い依存 - AI に渡さない境界: state mutation、課金、登録、権限、デプロイ、外部副作用 Lane evidence at finish: - `Codex`: active as orchestrator - `Claude`: unavailable in this run, `claude -p ...` timed out after 60 seconds - `Copilot`: fallback probe attempted first and timed out after 60 seconds - `GLM`: active via delegate bridge - `Gemini`: active via delegate bridge and used as fallback `fallback_used: true` `missing_lane: Claude` `fallback_provider: gemini-cli` `fallback_reason: claude -p timed out after 60 seconds; copilot -p timed out after 60 seconds` 次に進めるなら、`Cursorvers` のどの関数/API を agent tool 化し、どこに human approval gate を置くかを切ります。
